title: Instability_Control_by_Premixed_Pilot_Flames
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   ФPF1 = 1.1

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

**Table 1: Investigated cases without pilot ring injection. All cases with PFI injection were conducted with 7.5 kg/h PFI air mass flow rate at ΦPF1=1.1.**

| Cases           | Preheat temperature (K) | Outlet diameter (mm) | PFI injection (-) |
|-----------------|-------------------------|----------------------|-------------------|
| Baseline-550 K  | 550                     | 200                  | No                |
| PFI-550 K       | 550                     | 200                  | Yes               |
| PFI-550 K-65 mm | 550                     | 65                   | Yes               |
| PFI-300 K       | 300                     | 200                  | Yes               |

**Table 2: Investigated cases with pilot ring injection.  All test cases were conducted at a main air preheat temperature of 550 K.**

| Cases           | PFI air (kg/h) | PFI fuel (kg/h) | PR air (kg/h) | PR fuel (kg/h) |
|-----------------|----------------|------------------|---------------|-----------------|
| Baseline        | 1              | 0                | 0             | 0               |
| Premixed ring   | 1              | 0                | 15            | 0.96            |
| Diffusion ring  | 1              | 0                | 0             | 0.96            |
| Air ring        | 1              | 0                | 15            | 0               |

**4. Diagrams:**

**Fig. 2 PFI installation at the cone vertex and Pilot Ring at the combustor's dump plane. Insert shows the prototype of the PFI:**

*   Components: Central Recirculation Zone, Side Recirculation Zone, Pilot Ring, Pilot Flame, Swirl Burner, PFI Lance, Spark Plug, Fuel/Air
*   Labels: (1) sparkplug, (2) ground electrode, and (3) ceramic tube.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Main air mass flow rate: 200 kg/h
*   Preheated air temperature: 550 K
*   Non-preheated air temperature: 300 K
*   PFI air mass flow rate: 7.5 kg/h
*   Spark frequency: 110 Hz
*   Outlet diameter (full reflecting): 200 mm
*   Outlet diameter (low reflection): 65 mm
*   Acoustic reflection coefficient (r): <0.3
*   PFI Tube Length: 1.2 m
*   Air Mix Length: 1.5 m
*   Pilot ring diameter: 110 mm
*   Pilot ring tube diameter: 6 mm
*   Fuel injector diameter: 1 mm
*   Filter centered : 312 +/- 2 nm
*   ICDD camera: 1.4 m
*   Quartz glass combustion chamber: 0.1 m, 0.2m

title: Instability_Control_by_Premixed_Pilot_Flames
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   R = integral( p'(t) * (OH)'(t) dt) > dissipation losses (1)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 3**:
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Total equivalence ratio Phi_Total
        *   y-axis: Normalized RMS pressure level
    *   Curves:
        *   Baseline - 550K
        *   PFI - 550K
        *   PFI - 550K - 65mm
        *   PFI - 300K

*   **Fig. 4**:
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Time [sec]
        *   y-axis: Amplitude [Volt]
    *   Curves:
        *   microphone
        *   OH-signal
    * Key value: Phi_total=0.55

*   **Fig. 5**:
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Frequency [Hz]
        *   y-axis: Power spectrum
    *   Curves:
        *   PFI flame off
        *   PFI flame
    * Key value: Phi_total=0.55

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, and Parameters:**

*   Preheat air temperatures: 300 K and 550 K
*   Combustion outlet diameters: 65 mm and 200 mm
*   Low frequency pressure oscillations: 3-7 Hz
*   RMS pressure level for "baseline-550 K" case: very low near lean blowout at Phi_total <= 0.47
*   Thermo-acoustic instabilities for PFI-550 K: 0.47 <= Phi_total <= 0.6
*   Stable combustion for PFI-550 K: Phi_total > 0.6
*   PFI-300 K unstable condition: Phi_total > 0.67
*   Phi_total = 0.55 (Fig. 4 and 5)
*   Fundamental frequency (PFI-550 K): 110 Hz
*  PFI air mass flow rate of 7.5 kg/h at PPF1=1.1.

title: Instability_Control_by_Premixed_Pilot_Flames
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the extracted technical content from the provided images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 8:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Time [sec]
        *   y-axis: Pressure fluctuation
    *   Curves:
        *   Two plots showing pressure fluctuations vs. time, one for φTotal = 0.55 labeled "PFI flame off", PFI flame on"; one  φTotal=0.61 labeled "PFI flame off", PFI flame on"
    *   Key Values: Frequency of the PFI flame was 110 Hz.

*   **Figure 9:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Time [sec]
        *   y-axis: Pressure fluctuation
    *   Curve:
        *   Plot showing pressure fluctuations vs. time for φTotal = 0.66 labeled "PFI flame off", PFI flame on".
    *   Key values: Frequency of PFI flame was 110 Hz.

*   **Figure 11:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Total equivalence ratio φTotal
        *   y-axis: Normalized RMS pressure level
    *   Curves:
        *   RMS pressure labeled "PFI flame off" and  "PFI flame on" vs. total equivalence ratio, showing a marked increase at фTotal around 0.8 when PFI is off
    *   Notes: All RMS values are normalized on the background noise of the baseline case at φTotal = 0.45.


*   **Figure 12:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Total equivalence ratio φTotal
        *   y-axis: NOX [ppm] (dry, 15% O2)
    *   Curves:
        *   NOX emission vs. total equivalence ratio for three cases: PFI flame off, PFI flame, and Baseline-550K
    *   Baseline case demonstrates a very slight increase in NOX emissions, the PFI is negligble, and without it the emissions shoot up.

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**
OH-chemiluminescence between the side and central recirculation zones for PFI-550 K with and without PFI flame (shown in Figure 10)

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   φTotal = 0.55 (in Figure 8)
*   φTotal = 0.61 (in Figure 8)
*   110 Hz (frequency of the PFI flame)
*   φTotal = 0.66 (in Figure 9)
*   φTotal = 0.45 (baseline case for normalization in Figure 11)

title: Instability_Control_by_Premixed_Pilot_Flames
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 8]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Фtotal = total equivalence ratio calculated from Фmain and ФPFI

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 16**: Impact of different pilot ring injection cases on the main flame stabilization
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Main Equivalence Ratio (Фmain)
        *   y-axis: Normalized RMS Pressure Level Deviation
    *   Units: None specified for y-axis
    *   Curves: Diffusion Ring, Baseline, Premixed Ring, Air Ring
    *   Key Values:  The graph appears to show the relative stability for each pilot ring injection method and their range of operation.

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 17:** Mean flame heat release of the four injection configurations
    *   Components: Diffusion Ring, Baseline, Premixed Ring, Air Ring
    *   Label: Main = 0.51

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   TCCW = wall temperature of the combustion chamber
*   OH\* = OH-chemiluminescence
*   Фmain = main equivalence ratio of the main combustion
*   ФPFI = equivalence ratio of the flame produced by the pilot flame injector
*   Фpilot= equivalence ratio of the pilot flame
*   p' = pressure fluctuation within the combustion chamber
*   R = Rayleigh index
*   Фmain = 0.51
